title: silene otites subsp. baldaccii
intro: Silene otites subsp. baldaccii, also known as the Italian Catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe wounds and infections. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion. Silene otites subsp. baldaccii can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant require further research and validation.
